item.title: Fun fact if you don't live in Chicago
item.description: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/nHHMuBgIbmY Almost half of Chicago’s parking meters are owned by the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority. The other half are owned by a shadowy company called Redoma

Good morning imgur, let's fireball this bread.
Pippin (and wizard) tax. Today's message from Pippin: "whatchoo doing? I'm sorry. Uh oh."
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Now we all know about Flo
item.description: No one was more surprised than Christina Novak when the short online obituary she placed in the Kennebec Journal about her mom’s death went viral. “Let’s be clear. It’s a PSA and it’s no joke. It was written with sarcasm and relief, but it’s real,” Novak, 47, tells PEOPLE exclusively. “It was done in four sentences because I refused to spend another dollar on that woman.” https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/daughters-4-sentence-obit-mom-120000728.html
item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: Happy Star Trek Day from Litterbox! [OC]
item.description:
The pilot is funded, but the more we raise, the better we can make it! https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/litterboxanimation/pilot-episode?ref=f52qpp
One of the many exclusive rewards for Kickstarter campaign! https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/litterboxanimation/pilot-episode?ref=f52qpp (Sorry I keep going on about it, but after any limited edition thing, there's always people upset they missed it! Need to get the word out as much as possible!) :)

item.title: Annoying phone calls I've dealt with
item.description:
So way back in college I was working in a library on campus. One day while I was working I received a call from a scammer (vaguely foreign sounding accent, so possibly one of the ones over seas who target Americans because they think we'll be easier targets). He went into his spiel about how I had just won the Publisher's Clearing House sweepstakes (which I have NEVER entered in my life). They kept changing their story of how I was entered, including nonsense like "your employer entered your social security number into the sweepstakes and that's how you won". Just really idiotic BS like that. So I hemmed and hawed about accepting the prize before telling him I had enough money and didn't really need anymore money. I then told him to go ahead and donate the prize money to a charity for me. He seemed totally flabbergasted that I didn't want the money, and asked in a dumbfounded tone "you don't want the money?" I replied, "No. Just give it to charity for me." At this he absolutely lost his shit like I had dug up his favorite dead relative, spray painted graffiti on the corpse and tossed it in a drainage ditch. Finally he just screamed "FUCK YOU!" into the phone and hung up on me. I had considered stringing him along and fucking with him more, but I was at work and couldn't waste time doing that on the clock.
Again, this story takes place back in college. Back then I only had a little prepaid cellphone since I didn't need a smart phone at the time. At some point a gym in California started robocalling my number looking to speak to some guy I didn't know anything about other than the fact he probably lived in Nevada based on a few wrong number messages/texts meant for him. I think it was something to do with his gym membership fees or something along those lines. This was a fairly big problem for me since each time they called me and left a message it used up some of the limited number of minutes on my phone. Making matters worse, they would robocall me MULTIPLE times a day, all day, pretty much every day. I kept going thru their contact directory options to tell them they had the wrong number and to stop calling me. However, no matter how many times I contacted them about this they wouldn't stop calling me. And as it happens, that is technically a crime (at least in the US). So I eventually filed a formal complaint against them with the FCC. The calls stopped pretty abruptly a day or two later, and a week or two after that I received a letter in the mail from the FCC that basically said, "we have dealt with the problem." Albeit in a slightly less sinister government style of writing.
item.dc:creator: Othagovna
